bullets: Christians in Nigeria facing relentless jihadist killings, mass abductions, and militant attacks leaving thousands dead, wounded, or missing

A believer and fellow prisoners in North Korea held indefinitely in a political prison camp since 2001 following the discovery of a Bible, according to USCIRF

Afghan civilians killed and wounded — including women and children — in cross-border airstrikes and ground operations, with over 36 dead and 160 injured amid escalating tensions

Palestinians in Gaza and the occupied West Bank — including children — killed in ongoing strikes, with families facing worsening humanitarian conditions and shelter shortages
More than one million Lebanese displaced by conflict, thousands unable to return home due to destruction, as a fragile ceasefire framework takes hold

Somali civilians and communities in regions where ongoing military airstrikes and al-Shabaab militant activity continue to threaten lives and stability
Foreign nationals — including Malawians, Mozambicans, and Congolese — facing mob violence, forced displacement, and fear across multiple cities in South Africa

Students, teachers, and aid workers abducted or killed in attacks on schools and humanitarian convoys in South Sudan and Nigeria

Seafarers and civilian passengers endangered by drone and missile attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, with thousands stranded in the Persian Gulf
Residents of Bahrain affected by drone strikes on sovereign territory and living under ongoing regional military tensions
Civilians in southern Syria facing military incursions and displacement, with families fleeing artillery fire and repeated confrontations
Civilians in Afghanistan and Pakistan living amid escalating cross-border military operations, grief, and rising national tensions
Communities in Somalia's capital facing a deepening political crisis with confrontations between government forces and opposition threatening civilian safety
Residents across Mexico — including communities in tourist areas and local officials in southern states — living under the threat of gang violence and political killings
Six workers killed and their grieving families in Germany, following a shooting at a youth welfare facility
Three people seriously wounded — including a teenager — by a deliberate parcel bomb attack in Monaco
Thousands of protesters in Serbia seeking political accountability and democratic reform amid a prolonged national crisis
Civilians and security forces in Niger mourning those killed in a militant attack on critical national infrastructure
Russian civilians facing fuel shortages and rationing as energy infrastructure suffers repeated strikes
Residents of communities across the Philippines, Kenya, and South Sudan grieving loved ones killed in ambushes and security operations
Iraqi civilians and officials amid a dual crisis of militia disarmament deadlines and an anti-corruption crackdown involving dozens of detained officials
Residents of Chicago and the Bronx — including a five-year-old child — caught in ongoing gun violence affecting urban communities across the United States

Residents across Ghana's capital killed, displaced, and stranded by severe flooding with more rain forecast and emergency services stretched thin
Elderly and vulnerable people across Europe, especially in France, among over 1,300 excess deaths recorded during an extended and deadly heatwave
Communities in Uganda facing a potential Marburg virus case amid an already active Ebola response, raising fears of overlapping outbreaks

Residents of Canada's northern communities displaced by a rapidly expanding wildfire threatening homes across a wide area of Manitoba

Residents near active volcanoes in the Philippines and Italy monitoring ongoing eruptions and potential evacuation needs

Families in Russia's Kamchatka region threatened by rivers rising to dangerous flood levels with homes and infrastructure at risk
